name: Potatoes Au Gratin
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Spray a 1-quart round casserole dish with nonstick cooking spray.
3. Place potatoes in a 2-quart saucepan; add enough water to cover the potatoes. Bring to a boil over high heat. Cook, uncovered, about 10 minutes or until partially done. The potatoes should still be firm in the center. Drain and rinse in cold water until the potatoes are cool. Drain and set aside.
4. Meanwhile, melt 4 tablespoons margarine in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add flour, salt, and pepper, stirring until smooth. Gradually add milk, stirring constantly until the sauce is thickened. Add cheese, stirring until melted.
5. Cut potatoes crosswise into 1/4-inch-thick slices. Layer one third of the potatoes in the prepared dish. Top with one third of the onions and one third of the cheese sauce. Repeat layers twice, ending with the cheese sauce.
6. Melt remaining 2 tablespoons margarine. Combine cracker crumbs and melted margarine in a small bowl. Sprinkle evenly over casserole.
7. Bake, uncovered, for 35-40 minutes or until hot and bubbly and potatoes are tender.
8. Makes 4-6 servings.
